question.text: As per SEBI (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993, which of the following entities can apply for registration as a Debenture Trustee?

answer.text: Any individual with relevant experience in the securities market
A partnership firm with adequate infrastructure
A scheduled bank carrying on commercial activity, a public financial institution, an insurance company, or a body corporate
Only a public financial institution or a scheduled commercial bank

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the regulations, no person shall be entitled to act as a debenture trustee unless they are (a) a scheduled bank carrying on commercial activity, (b) a public financial institution as per section 2(72) of Companies Act 2013, (c) an insurance company, or (d) a body corporate as per section 2(11) of Companies Act 2013. **The Trap:** The last option excludes insurance companies and body corporates, which are also eligible. Individuals and partnership firms are not eligible. **Cross-Exam Context:** Eligibility criteria for various intermediaries are frequently tested in NISM Series III-A exam. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The eligibility is broader than just banks and PFIs -- insurance companies and body corporates also qualify.
